description_long: This is a two-story half-timbered house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered, mossy tiles. The upper facade features a grid of dark wooden beams set against light plaster, with multiple evenly spaced wooden shuttered windows. The lower portion of the house is constructed from rough stone, giving it a sturdy base and rustic appearance. A small wooden balcony or porch is attached to one side, partially enclosed by a simple railing. The house has no visible doors from most angles, but the presence of a porch suggests an entryway on the ground floor. The overall design evokes traditional European or medieval urban architecture, suitable for a dense neighborhood or historical street.
description: A two-story, half-timbered house with a gable roof, stone base, and shuttered windows, featuring a small wooden porch. The exterior combines weathered plaster, dark wood beams, and mossy roof tiles for a rustic, old-world look.
description_view_0: Side view showing the gable end, plain plaster wall with timber framing, and a partial view of the wooden porch.
description_view_1: Front view displaying symmetrical rows of shuttered windows, half-timbered facade, and the mossy tiled roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side gable view with visible timber framing, shuttered windows, and a clearer look at the wooden porch structure.
description_view_3: Rear view with more stonework at the base, symmetrical window arrangement, and the porch extending from the side.
attribute_entryway_design: Ground-level entry with a small wooden porch and railing, likely featuring a simple wooden door.
